mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal income and housing starts indicates a synchronized contraction in consumer spending and construction investment, which directly reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a bivariate regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero at p > 0.10 over a 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals resilient manufacturing output that offsets higher financing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns, with the conditional predictive accuracy dropping below 45% over a 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days regardless of short-term market noise.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no significant positive association with 60-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, anticipated central bank tightening and margin compression force equity valuations lower, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate declines confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with the interaction term in a predictive model losing statistical significance at p > 0.05 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production combined with falling real personal income signal a broad-based economic slowdown that triggers risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling INDPRO and RSAFS does not predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy falling below 40% during confirmed downturn periods.
